name: Refrigerator Door Shelf Bin
description: Genuine OEM lower door shelf bin for side-by-side refrigerators. Clear design with white trim. Tool-free snap installation. Approximately 14.25 inches wide and 6 inches deep.
installation:
Step 1: Open the refrigerator door fully.
Step 2: Tilt the old bin upward to release from door clips.
Step 3: Align the new bin with the mounting tabs.
Step 4: Press firmly until it snaps into place.
safetyNote: No power shutoff needed.

name: Refrigerator Ice Maker Assembly
description: Complete ice maker assembly for Whirlpool refrigerators. Includes ice mold, ejector, shut-off arm, and wire harness. Most common fix when the ice maker stops producing ice.
installation:
Step 1: ⚠️ Unplug the refrigerator and turn off the water supply.
Step 2: Open the freezer and locate the ice maker on the upper-left wall.
Step 3: Disconnect the wire harness by pressing the locking tabs.
Step 4: Remove the mounting screws using a 1/4-inch nut driver.
Step 5: Slide the ice maker upward and out.
Step 6: Install the new ice maker in reverse order.
Step 7: Restore water and power. Allow 24 hours for first ice.
safetyNote: Unplug and shut off water before replacing.

name: Refrigerator Water Inlet Valve
description: Dual inlet valve controlling water to the ice maker and water dispenser. If neither is getting water, check this first.
installation:
Step 1: ⚠️ Unplug the refrigerator and shut off the water supply.
Step 2: Pull the refrigerator away from the wall.
Step 3: Remove the lower back panel.
Step 4: Disconnect the water supply line — have a towel ready.
Step 5: Disconnect all electrical connectors.
Step 6: Remove the mounting screws and swap in the new valve.
Step 7: Reconnect all lines, restore water and power, check for leaks.
safetyNote: Unplug and shut off water. Have a towel ready for residual water.

name: EveryDrop Refrigerator Water Filter 1
description: NSF certified filter reducing 24 contaminants. Replace every 6 months or 200 gallons. A clogged filter is the #1 cause of slow water.
installation:
Step 1: Locate the filter in the upper-right inside the refrigerator.
Step 2: Turn the old filter counterclockwise 1/4 turn and pull out.
Step 3: Insert the new filter and turn clockwise until locked.
Step 4: Flush 4 gallons of water through the dispenser.
Step 5: Reset the filter indicator light.
safetyNote: No power shutoff needed.

name: Refrigerator Evaporator Fan Motor
description: Fan motor that circulates cold air through the refrigerator and freezer. If the compressor runs but the fridge is warm, this motor is likely the cause.
installation:
Step 1: ⚠️ Unplug the refrigerator.
Step 2: Remove all freezer contents and shelves.
Step 3: Remove the rear panel in the freezer.
Step 4: Disconnect the wire harness and remove mounting screws.
Step 5: Transfer the fan blade to the new motor, reinstall, reconnect harness.
safetyNote: Always unplug before accessing internal components.

name: Refrigerator Defrost Heater Assembly
description: Defrost heater that melts frost buildup on evaporator coils. Excessive frost on the freezer back wall is the classic sign this part has failed.
installation:
Step 1: ⚠️ Unplug refrigerator.
Step 2: Remove freezer contents and rear panel.
Step 3: Defrost existing ice with a hair dryer — do not puncture coils.
Step 4: Disconnect heater connectors and slide old heater out.
Step 5: Install new heater, reconnect, replace panels.
symptoms: fridge section warm but freezer fine
safetyNote: Unplug. Keep hair dryer away from electrical connections.

name: Refrigerator Compressor Start Relay
description: Start relay that helps the compressor start. If you hear clicking every few minutes and the fridge is warm, shake the relay — if it rattles, it is faulty.
installation:
Step 1: ⚠️ Unplug the refrigerator.
Step 2: Pull it away from the wall and locate the compressor.
Step 3: Pull the start relay straight off the compressor terminal.
Step 4: Plug in the new relay.
Step 5: Restore power and wait 10-15 minutes.
safetyNote: Always unplug before accessing the compressor area.

name: Refrigerator Ice Bucket Assembly
description: Ice storage bucket/bin that sits below the ice maker in the freezer. If the bucket is cracked or the auger motor inside is faulty, ice won't dispense properly.
installation:
Step 1: Open the freezer door.
Step 2: Pull the ice bucket straight out toward you.
Step 3: Insert the new bucket, sliding it back into position until it seats firmly.
safetyNote: No power shutoff needed.

name: Refrigerator Glass Shelf Assembly
description: Tempered glass shelf with white plastic frame for the fresh food compartment. Tool-free installation — simply remove the old shelf and slide in the new one.
installation:
Step 1: Slide the broken shelf forward and lift out.
Step 2: Slide the new shelf into the mounting grooves on the refrigerator walls.
Step 3: Lower into position until it sits level.
safetyNote: No power shutoff needed. Handle glass carefully.

name: Refrigerator Condenser Fan Motor
description: Condenser fan motor located at the back/bottom of the refrigerator. Cools the condenser coils and compressor. If the fridge runs warm and the compressor is hot to the touch, this fan may have stopped working.
installation:
Step 1: ⚠️ Unplug refrigerator.
Step 2: Remove the rear access panel at the bottom back.
Step 3: Disconnect the fan motor wire harness.
Step 4: Remove mounting screws and pull out old motor with blade.
Step 5: Install new motor, reconnect harness, replace panel.
safetyNote: Always unplug before accessing the back panel.

name: Refrigerator Door Gasket Seal
description: Door seal that keeps cold air inside the refrigerator. If you notice condensation around the door or the fridge runs constantly, the gasket may be failing. Test: close the door on a dollar bill — if it slides out easily, replace the gasket.
installation:
Step 1: Starting at one corner, pull the old gasket out of the channel.
Step 2: If stiff, warm with a hair dryer to make it pliable.
Step 3: Starting at the top center, press the new gasket into the channel.
Step 4: Work around all edges ensuring no gaps.
Step 5: Check the dollar bill seal test.
safetyNote: No power shutoff needed.

name: GE Refrigerator Water Inlet Valve
description: Genuine GE water inlet valve for refrigerators with ice and water dispensers. Controls water flow to both the ice maker and water dispenser. Compatible with many GE Profile and GE Cafe models.
installation:
Step 1: ⚠️ Unplug the refrigerator and shut off water.
Step 2: Access the valve through the lower rear access panel.
Step 3: Disconnect the supply line and electrical connectors.
Step 4: Replace the valve and reconnect everything.
Step 5: Check for leaks before pushing the fridge back.
safetyNote: Unplug and shut off water supply before replacing.

name: GE Refrigerator Ice Maker Assembly
description: Complete ice maker replacement for GE side-by-side and bottom-freezer refrigerators. Includes all components — just swap the assembly and reconnect.
installation:
Step 1: ⚠️ Unplug refrigerator and shut off water supply.
Step 2: Locate the ice maker in the freezer compartment.
Step 3: Remove the mounting screws and disconnect the wiring harness.
Step 4: Install the new ice maker in reverse order.
Step 5: Allow 24 hours for first ice cycle.
symptoms: GE ice maker not making ice
safetyNote: Unplug and shut off water before replacing.

name: Samsung Refrigerator Water Filter
description: Genuine Samsung HAF-CIN water filter. NSF certified, reduces chlorine, lead, and contaminants. Replace every 6 months. Compatible with most Samsung French door and side-by-side models.
installation:
Step 1: Locate the filter in the upper right of the refrigerator.
Step 2: Push in and turn counterclockwise to remove old filter.
Step 3: Insert new filter and turn clockwise until locked.
Step 4: Run 2 gallons of water to flush.
Step 5: Hold the Ice/Water button for 3 seconds to reset the filter light.
safetyNote: No power shutoff needed.

name: Samsung Refrigerator Ice Maker
description: Genuine Samsung ice maker assembly for French door refrigerators. Known issue: Samsung ice maker failures are common in RF28 and similar models. This is the OEM replacement, with improved reliability.
installation:
Step 1: ⚠️ Unplug refrigerator and shut off water.
Step 2: Open the freezer door and locate the ice maker on the upper wall.
Step 3: Remove the ice bucket first.
Step 4: Disconnect the wiring harness and remove mounting screws.
Step 5: Install new ice maker, reconnect wiring, replace ice bucket.
Step 6: Plug in and allow 24 hours for first ice cycle.
safetyNote: Unplug and shut off water before replacing.

name: Frigidaire Refrigerator Ice Maker Assembly
description: Complete ice maker assembly for Frigidaire side-by-side and top-freezer refrigerators. Includes all internal components — replace the entire assembly rather than individual parts.
installation:
Step 1: ⚠️ Unplug refrigerator and shut off water.
Step 2: Locate the ice maker in the upper freezer area.
Step 3: Remove the wiring harness connector and mounting screws.
Step 4: Slide out the old ice maker and slide in the new one.
Step 5: Reconnect wiring and restore power and water.
safetyNote: Unplug and shut off water before replacing.

name: Dishwasher Door Latch Assembly
description: Door latch and switch assembly. If the dishwasher door does not click shut or the dishwasher will not start, the latch or its internal switch has likely failed.
installation:
Step 1: ⚠️ Disconnect power at the breaker.
Step 2: Remove inner panel screws around the perimeter.
Step 3: Separate the inner and outer door panels.
Step 4: Photograph wiring before disconnecting.
Step 5: Remove old latch and install new one.
Step 6: Reassemble panels.
safetyNote: Disconnect power at the circuit breaker before opening the door panel.

name: Dishwasher Upper Spray Arm
description: Upper spray arm distributing water to top rack dishes. If the top rack isn't getting clean, check this first — holes may be clogged or the arm may not spin freely.
installation:
Step 1: Pull out the upper rack.
Step 2: Locate the spray arm below the rack.
Step 3: Turn the center nut counterclockwise to remove.
Step 4: Install new spray arm and turn nut clockwise.
Step 5: Verify the arm spins freely.
safetyNote: No power shutoff needed.

name: Dishwasher Lower Spray Arm
description: Lower spray arm responsible for cleaning bottom rack dishes. A cracked or clogged spray arm is a very common cause of poor washing performance.
installation:
Step 1: Remove the lower dish rack.
Step 2: Pull the spray arm straight up to remove.
Step 3: Install the new arm by pressing down until it snaps.
Step 4: Verify free rotation.
safetyNote: No power shutoff needed.

name: Dishwasher Circulation Pump and Motor
description: Main circulation pump and motor that drives water through the spray arms. If the dishwasher hums but no water sprays, this pump may have failed.
installation:
Step 1: ⚠️ Disconnect power and water supply.
Step 2: Remove lower rack, spray arm, and filter.
Step 3: Remove sump cover.
Step 4: Disconnect wire harness and water hoses.
Step 5: Rotate pump counterclockwise to unlock and remove.
Step 6: Install new pump in reverse.
safetyNote: Disconnect power and water supply before accessing the sump.

name: Dishwasher Drain Pump
description: Drain pump removing water at the end of each cycle. Standing water in the bottom after a cycle is the clear sign this pump has failed or is blocked.
installation:
Step 1: ⚠️ Disconnect power.
Step 2: Remove lower rack, spray arm, and filter.
Step 3: Clear debris from the sump area.
Step 4: Disconnect wire harness and drain hose from the drain pump.
Step 5: Remove mounting screws and swap in the new pump.
safetyNote: Always disconnect power before accessing internal components.

name: Dishwasher Filter Assembly
description: Fine mesh filter catching food particles. Clean monthly. A dirty filter is the #1 cause of poor cleaning and drainage in Whirlpool dishwashers.
installation:
Step 1: Remove the lower rack.
Step 2: Turn the cylindrical upper filter counterclockwise and lift out.
Step 3: Remove the flat lower filter.
Step 4: Rinse under warm water with a soft brush.
Step 5: Reinstall lower filter first, then twist upper filter clockwise until locked.
safetyNote: No power shutoff needed.

name: Dishwasher Door Gasket Seal
description: Door seal preventing water from leaking out around the dishwasher door. If water pools on the floor during cycles, inspect the gasket for cracks.
installation:
Step 1: Pull the old gasket out of the channel around the door.
Step 2: Clean the channel.
Step 3: Press the new gasket in starting at the top center, working around.
Step 4: Run a short cycle and check for leaks.
symptoms: water on floor in front of dishwasher
safetyNote: No power shutoff needed. Run a test cycle after installation.

name: Dishwasher Detergent Dispenser Assembly
description: Detergent dispenser that releases soap and rinse aid at the correct time in the wash cycle. If detergent is left in the dispenser at the end of the cycle, the dispenser door is stuck or the wax motor has failed.
installation:
Step 1: ⚠️ Disconnect power.
Step 2: Remove the inner door panel.
Step 3: Photograph the wiring before disconnecting.
Step 4: Remove mounting screws and swap in the new dispenser.
Step 5: Reconnect wiring and reassemble the door.
safetyNote: Disconnect power before opening the inner door panel.

name: Dishwasher Float Switch Assembly
description: Float switch that detects overfill conditions and prevents flooding. If the dishwasher fills with water but then stops, or conversely won't fill at all, the float switch may be stuck or broken.
installation:
Step 1: ⚠️ Disconnect power.
Step 2: Remove the lower rack and the float cap from the bottom of the tub.
Step 3: Reach under and disconnect the wires from the float switch.
Step 4: Unscrew the switch and install the new one.
Step 5: Reinstall the float cap.
safetyNote: Disconnect power before accessing the float switch.

name: Dishwasher Heating Element
description: Heating element that raises water temperature during the wash cycle and dries dishes in the dry cycle. If dishes are coming out wet or the water seems cold, the heating element may have burned out.
installation:
Step 1: ⚠️ Disconnect power.
Step 2: Remove the lower rack and filter.
Step 3: At the bottom of the tub, locate the heating element ends.
Step 4: Disconnect the wires from outside the tub (rear access).
Step 5: Remove the mounting nuts from inside the tub and pull out the element.
Step 6: Install new element in reverse.
safetyNote: Disconnect power before accessing the heating element.

name: Dishwasher Thermal Fuse
description: Safety device that cuts power to the dishwasher if it overheats. If the dishwasher suddenly stops working completely with no power at all, a blown thermal fuse is a common cause.
installation:
Step 1: ⚠️ Disconnect power.
Step 2: Remove the inner door panel.
Step 3: Locate the thermal fuse (small component with two wires).
Step 4: Test with a multimeter — no continuity means it is blown.
Step 5: Disconnect wires and swap in the new fuse.
safetyNote: Disconnect power. Test with a multimeter before replacing to confirm diagnosis.

name: Frigidaire Dishwasher Drain Pump
description: Genuine Frigidaire drain pump for dishwashers. If water remains standing in the tub after a cycle, this pump is the most likely cause after ruling out a clogged filter and drain hose.
installation:
Step 1: ⚠️ Disconnect power.
Step 2: Remove the lower rack and filter assembly.
Step 3: Clear debris from the sump.
Step 4: Disconnect wire harness and drain hose from the pump.
Step 5: Remove old pump and install new one.
Step 6: Reconnect all connections.
safetyNote: Disconnect power before accessing internal components.

name: Refrigerator Upper Door Shelf Bin
description: Upper door shelf bin, pairs with lower bin PS11752778. Tool-free snap installation.
installation:
Step 1: Tilt old bin upward to release.
Step 2: Align new bin with mounting tabs.
Step 3: Press until it snaps into place.
safetyNote: No power shutoff needed.

name: Dishwasher Control Board
description: Electronic control board managing all dishwasher functions. If buttons don't respond or error codes appear, rule out simpler causes first before replacing this board.
installation:
Step 1: ⚠️ Disconnect power at the circuit breaker.
Step 2: Remove the inner door panel.
Step 3: Photograph all wire harness connections.
Step 4: Disconnect connectors and remove mounting screws.
Step 5: Install new board and reconnect all wires as photographed.
safetyNote: Disconnect power at the circuit breaker. Photograph all wiring before disconnecting.
